title: Micro-retreat planner for burned-out founders
one_liner: A weekend reset planner that turns founder stress signals into tiny, bookable recovery itineraries.
target_group: Solo founders and small startup teams
problem: Founders often wait until exhaustion becomes severe before planning rest, and generic travel planning feels like more work.
solution: Collect constraints, stress level, budget, and location, then generate a realistic two-day retreat plan with booking links and recovery goals.
uniqueness: Treats rest like a strategic sprint retrospective instead of wellness content.
ai_role: AI interviews the user, creates itineraries, and adapts them to budget, weather, and energy level.
swot_strength: Clear emotional pain and strong premium positioning.
swot_weakness: Travel fulfillment can become operationally messy.
swot_opportunity: Could expand into team offsites and founder communities.
swot_threat: Travel platforms may copy lightweight planning flows.

title: AI naming kiln
one_liner: A naming workshop that stress-tests product names against positioning, memorability, domain fit, and visual identity.
problem: Name generation tools produce lists, but do not help teams decide which names are strategically strong.
solution: Generate candidate names, score them against strategy, and create a decision sheet with risks and naming territories.
uniqueness: Focuses on judgment and tradeoffs, not just generation.
ai_role: AI proposes territories, creates names, critiques them, and writes rationale.
swot_strength: Fast MVP and obvious agency workflow.
swot_opportunity: Can sell templates, audits, and brand sprint packages.
swot_threat: Generic AI chat can satisfy casual users.
revival_strategy: Position as a decision support tool for agencies rather than a public naming toy.

title: Local service price radar
one_liner: A lightweight dashboard that tracks public price ranges for plumbers, cleaners, tutors, and other local services.
problem: Customers do not know fair local prices, and service operators do not know where they sit in the market.
solution: Aggregate public listings and user-submitted quotes into local range cards with confidence levels.
uniqueness: Turns messy quote hunting into a local pricing intelligence feed.
ai_role: AI normalizes service descriptions and extracts comparable price signals.
swot_strength: Strong buyer pain and useful market data.
swot_weakness: Data quality and locality are hard.
swot_opportunity: Could become lead-gen or SaaS for operators.
rejection_reason: Data acquisition is too heavy for the first offline MVP.
revival_strategy: Start with one city and one service category using manual quote collection.

title: Tiny grant matcher for schools
one_liner: A grant discovery assistant that matches small classroom needs with local funds and writes first-pass applications.
problem: Small grants are scattered, and teachers do not have time to search or draft applications.
solution: Collect classroom needs, match available grants, and generate a concise application draft.
uniqueness: Targets small underused grants rather than large institutional funding.
ai_role: AI summarizes grant criteria, matches fit, and drafts applications.
swot_strength: Clear social value and repeatable workflow.
swot_threat: Grant databases may add AI matching.
revival_strategy: Validate willingness to pay through school foundations and sponsors.

title: Recipe archaeology
one_liner: A tool that turns old family recipe scans into modern, testable recipes with story notes and shopping lists.
problem: Old recipes are handwritten, incomplete, and hard to share with younger family members.
solution: Upload scans or text, reconstruct quantities and steps, and produce a clean recipe card with optional family notes.
uniqueness: Combines OCR cleanup with cultural memory preservation.
ai_role: AI interprets ambiguous recipe notes and proposes tested modern instructions.
swot_opportunity: Print products, family archive bundles, and holiday campaigns.
swot_threat: Photo and note apps can absorb the use case.

title: B2B apology writer
one_liner: A calm incident communication assistant for SaaS teams that turns messy outage notes into customer-ready updates.
problem: During incidents, teams know what happened but struggle to write clear, accountable updates quickly.
solution: Convert internal notes into status updates, customer emails, and postmortems with tone controls.
uniqueness: Narrowly focused on apology, accountability, and repair language.
ai_role: AI structures timelines, removes blamey phrasing, and drafts channel-specific messages.
swot_weakness: Needs trust before teams use it in real incidents.
swot_opportunity: Integrations with status pages and support tools.
swot_threat: Incident platforms can add similar templates.

title: Podcast sponsor fit scanner
one_liner: A sponsorship matcher that scores podcasts and brands by audience promise, tone, and likely conversion fit.
target_group: Small podcast networks and niche brands
problem: Podcast sponsorship fit is judged manually and often wastes outreach cycles.
solution: Analyze show descriptions, transcripts, and brand positioning to produce ranked sponsor matches.
uniqueness: Prioritizes brand-show resonance over generic audience size.
ai_role: AI extracts audience themes and writes outreach angles.
swot_strength: Clear workflow for creators and brands.
swot_weakness: Needs reliable audience and performance data.
swot_threat: Existing ad networks have relationship advantages.
rejection_reason: Buyer side is not yet precise enough.
revival_strategy: Interview podcast producers before building brand features.

title: Inbox for future selves
one_liner: A personal reflection inbox that stores decisions, fears, and assumptions, then resurfaces them when outcomes arrive.
problem: People forget what they believed before important decisions, making learning from outcomes shallow.
solution: Capture decision memos and schedule future reviews with prompts for what changed.
uniqueness: Treats personal growth as a lightweight prediction ledger.
ai_role: AI asks sharper reflection questions and compares old assumptions to outcomes.
swot_strength: Simple and emotionally sticky for some users.
swot_weakness: May be too broad for a clear buyer.
swot_threat: Journaling apps and Notion templates compete.
revival_strategy: Narrow to executive coaches or founder decision logs.

title: Tiny compliance translator
one_liner: A plain-language compliance explainer that turns policy changes into practical checklists for micro-businesses.
problem: Small businesses miss regulatory changes because official guidance is dense and not workflow-specific.
solution: Summarize changes by business type and generate a checklist of practical next steps.
uniqueness: Focuses on very small businesses that cannot justify consultants.
ai_role: AI translates official text into role-specific tasks and flags uncertainty.
swot_weakness: Legal accuracy and liability risk are severe.
swot_opportunity: Partner with accountants or chambers of commerce.
swot_threat: Regulated advice boundaries can block trust.
rejection_reason: Too much legal liability for the first product.
revival_strategy: Reframe as an internal checklist builder for accountants, not direct legal advice.

title: Festival food line optimizer
one_liner: A vendor operations tool that predicts queue pressure and recommends menu, staffing, and prep changes.
problem: Food vendors lose sales when lines grow or prep bottlenecks hit peak windows.
solution: Use simple inputs about menu speed, staffing, and historical demand to simulate queue pressure.
uniqueness: Targets small vendors instead of large venue operators.
ai_role: AI converts messy vendor knowledge into practical operational recommendations.
swot_opportunity: Could expand into popups and farmers markets.
swot_threat: Operators may resist data entry during events.
rejection_reason: Interesting but not aligned with current team access.
revival_strategy: Revive only if a vendor partner appears.

title: Sales call objection atlas
one_liner: A review board for sales calls that clusters objections and suggests positioning experiments.
problem: Small teams hear objections but do not systematically turn them into product or messaging experiments.
solution: Upload call notes, cluster objections, and produce weekly experiments for positioning and product proof.
uniqueness: Connects call review directly to experiment design.
ai_role: AI extracts objections, detects patterns, and suggests tests.
swot_strength: Strong B2B pain with direct revenue tie.
swot_weakness: Needs privacy-sensitive handling of sales notes.
swot_opportunity: Integrations with CRMs and call recorders.
swot_threat: Sales intelligence suites can move into this.

title: Dead-simple warranty vault
one_liner: A home inventory app that stores warranties, receipts, and maintenance reminders with zero spreadsheet work.
target_group: Homeowners and renters with expensive appliances
problem: Receipts and warranties disappear exactly when people need repairs or claims.
solution: Capture receipts, parse warranty dates, and remind users before coverage or maintenance windows expire.
uniqueness: Less inventory database, more practical claim readiness.
ai_role: AI parses documents and extracts warranty terms into reminders.
swot_strength: Simple consumer pain and clear utility.
swot_threat: Cloud storage and finance apps can add receipt reminders.
